entries.statement: All signal construction and execution must use only information available no later than the decision bar close; future bars are inadmissible.
entries.basis: research_constitution_v3.0.yaml execution invariants and meta_knowledge_policy Tier1 examples
entries.scope: All discovery, evaluation, packaging, and forward review work in this lineage
entries.overlap_guard: Not derived from overlapping historical results and cannot be weakened by same-snapshot findings.
entries.challenge_rule: May change only through a major constitution revision.

entries.statement: All timestamps, folds, daily returns, and cross-timeframe joins are defined in UTC.
entries.scope: All lineage artifacts and execution logic
entries.overlap_guard: Operational invariant; not inferable from snapshot performance.
entries.challenge_rule: May change only through a major constitution revision.

entries.statement: Signals are generated at bar close and filled at the next bar open; same-bar execution is forbidden.
entries.scope: All candidate implementations and forward reviews
entries.overlap_guard: Execution semantics are fixed ex ante and cannot be tuned from overlapping data.
entries.challenge_rule: May change only through a major constitution revision.

entries.statement: Missing bars may be logged but must not be repaired or imputed with synthetic data.
entries.scope: Raw data ingestion, validation, and all downstream evaluations
entries.overlap_guard: Data-integrity invariant; not subject to empirical override.
entries.challenge_rule: May change only through a major constitution revision.

entries.statement: Layering is a hypothesis to be justified by measurement and ablation, not a default system design choice.
entries.basis: research_constitution_v3.0.yaml meta_knowledge_policy Tier2 example and candidate design philosophy
entries.scope: Future candidate design and review burden only
entries.overlap_guard: Cannot be used to forbid blind discovery on overlapping data; it only raises the evidence burden for added layers.
entries.challenge_rule: May be challenged by constitution amendment or by broader non-overlapping evidence on design complexity.

entries.statement: Microstructure-heavy effects are not presumed to be mainline for this swing-horizon lineage unless broader evidence later elevates them.
entries.scope: Budgeting and burden of proof for future designs in this lineage
entries.overlap_guard: Cannot exclude measurement of microstructure on overlapping data; it only prevents treating it as the default design center.
entries.challenge_rule: May be challenged by non-overlapping forward evidence or a major constitution revision.

entries.statement: Prefer the smallest defensible design; additional tunables or layers must earn their place via measured signal and later ablation.
entries.basis: research_constitution_v3.0.yaml complexity law and D1c design philosophy
entries.overlap_guard: Cannot be used to suppress blind measurement; it only affects preference when signal quality is otherwise close.
entries.challenge_rule: May be challenged by constitution amendment or by durable forward evidence showing justified complexity.

entries.statement: Within snapshot_20260318 discovery, dominant price structure was multi-bar continuation rather than next-bar persistence; next-bar persistence was weak.
entries.scope: Advisory note for this session only
entries.overlap_guard: May not narrow blind discovery on the same historical snapshot in a future seed session.
entries.challenge_rule: Re-measure on appended forward data or on a non-overlapping seed snapshot before reuse as an empirical prior.

entries.statement: Range position and volatility-normalized return states were generally cleaner than raw signed momentum in fast and slow channels within discovery.
entries.scope: Advisory note for this session only
entries.overlap_guard: Cannot be elevated to a Tier2 design prior on overlapping data.
entries.challenge_rule: Must be revalidated on appended forward data or a distinct seed snapshot.

entries.statement: Order-flow channels were the least redundant family measured; the strongest cross-timeframe interaction was slow trend permission combined with faster order-flow.
entries.scope: Advisory note for this session only
entries.overlap_guard: Cannot narrow blind rediscovery on the same snapshot; this is an overlapping empirical finding.
entries.challenge_rule: Must be rechecked on appended forward data before influencing governance beyond advisory weight.

entries.statement: Volume level was primarily a future-magnitude state variable; directional content was weaker than magnitude content across measured timeframes.
entries.scope: Advisory note for this session only
entries.overlap_guard: Cannot be used as a hard exclusion or search restriction on overlapping data.
entries.challenge_rule: Re-test on appended forward data or a non-overlapping seed snapshot.

entries.statement: Calendar day-of-week and weekend effects were weak; intraday UTC-session timing effects were stronger but still secondary.
entries.scope: Advisory note for this session only
entries.overlap_guard: Cannot be elevated into a structural prior on overlapping data.
entries.challenge_rule: Re-measure on appended data before reuse.

entries.statement: Intraday downside-tail and crisis rebound effects were measurable but sparse and event-driven rather than broad baseline structure.
entries.scope: Advisory note for this session only
entries.overlap_guard: Cannot be used to pre-approve event-driven designs on overlapping data.
entries.challenge_rule: Must be retested on fresh appended data before operational use.

entries.statement: No main candidate configuration survived the 50 bps discovery hard-constraint filter in this seed session.
entries.basis: D1d3 aggregate and D1e1 hard-constraint filter
entries.scope: Advisory note for this session only
entries.overlap_guard: Cannot be used to skip blind discovery on the same snapshot; it only records the outcome of this session.
entries.challenge_rule: A new conclusion requires non-overlapping data or a constitution-approved methodology change.

entries.statement: Both tested multi-layer candidates failed ablation review in discovery: removing the faster flow layer improved Calmar, so added complexity did not earn its place.
entries.scope: Advisory note for this session only
entries.overlap_guard: Cannot be generalized into a permanent ban on layering; this note applies only to the tested designs on overlapping data.
entries.challenge_rule: Future layered designs must be re-tested and can overturn this note on fresh data.

entries.statement: The fast 15m normalized-continuation design was too turnover-heavy and cost-sensitive to survive discovery hard constraints at 50 bps.
entries.basis: D1d3 aggregate and D1e1 hard-constraint filter
entries.scope: Advisory note for this session only
entries.overlap_guard: Cannot be used as a permanent exclusion of 15m designs on overlapping data.
entries.challenge_rule: Retest on appended forward data or a distinct snapshot before reuse as a design veto.
